mnemonics.title: Remember p and q formulas
mnemonics.content:
p values: LEFT = -dx, RIGHT = +dx, BOTTOM = -dy, TOP = +dy
q values: LEFT = x1-xmin, RIGHT = xmax-x1, BOTTOM = y1-ymin, TOP = ymax-y1
Mnemonic: 'L RNB T' - Left Negative dx, Right dx, Negative dy, dy for Top

mnemonics.title: Remember u1 and u2 update rules
mnemonics.content:
For p < 0: u1 = max(u1, q/p) - Entering edge pushes u1 UP
For p > 0: u2 = min(u2, q/p) - Exiting edge pushes u2 DOWN
Mnemonic: 'Enter Goes Up, Exit Goes Down'

mnemonics.title: Remember rejection condition
mnemonics.content:
If p=0 and q<0, then REJECT (line parallel and outside)
Mnemonic: 'Zero Parallel, Negative Outside' - PN (parallel, reject when negative)

acronyms.usage: p = (p1, p2, p3, p4) = (-dx, dx, -dy, dy) for left, right, bottom, top edges

acronyms.expansion: Parameter values representing distance from point to edge
acronyms.usage: q = (q1, q2, q3, q4) = (x1-xmin, xmax-x1, y1-ymin, ymax-y1)
u1 = max of all q/p values where p<0, initially 0
u2 = min of all q/p values where p>0, initially 1

visualTips.visualization: Visualize p as the 'direction' the line moves relative to each edge (+ means moving toward positive x/y direction), and q as the 'distance' from start point to each edge
Think of the line as a number line from 0 to 1, where u=0 is start, u=1 is end, and clipping 'cuts off' portions from the beginning or end
Imagine u1 as the 'front door' that can only open wider (max), and u2 as the 'back door' that can only close more (min)

analogies.analogy: Think of the clipping window as a store window. The line is a view through the window. u1 is where you start looking, u2 is where you stop. If u1 ≤ u2, there's something to see; otherwise, the view is blocked.
p values are like directional signs at each edge: p<0 means you're entering from that side (entering edge), p>0 means you're leaving (exiting edge)
If the line is parallel to an edge (p=0) and on the wrong side (q<0), it's like trying to park in a spot that's already occupied - reject it!
